questions: Identify the specific macOS release described as having a 'fresh new design' that enables users to get automatic translations in calls and messages and recover deleted passwords. Based on the product ecosystem associated with this specific software generation, list the corresponding operating system version names designated for iPad, Apple Watch, and Apple Vision Pro. Additionally, identify the specific 'Ultra' wearable model and the 'Pro' iPhone model listed in this lineup.

Consult the Mac User Guide to identify the specific macOS version explicitly described as having a 'fresh new design' and the ability to 'view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop' alongside 'Apple Intelligence' integration. Once this operating system version is identified, provide a summary that includes the new operational capabilities listed for Spotlight, the specific automatic language features added to calls and messages, and the stated functionality for recovering deleted credentials.

According to the Mac User Guide for the operating system that incorporates 'Apple Intelligence', what specific functionality allows users to operate their iPhone directly from the computer's desktop, and what mechanism is provided within the privacy and security settings to restore accidentally removed authentication credentials?

rounds.question: Identify the specific macOS release described as having a 'fresh new design' that enables users to get automatic translations in calls and messages and recover deleted passwords. Based on the product ecosystem associated with this specific software generation, list the corresponding operating system version names designated for iPad, Apple Watch, and Apple Vision Pro. Additionally, identify the specific 'Ultra' wearable model and the 'Pro' iPhone model listed in this lineup.
checklist_width: Apple Vision Pro Operating System: visionOS 26
checklist_depth: Logic Proof: Identified as the OS with 'fresh new design', 'automatic translations', and password recovery features.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify 'macOS Tahoe' solely through its unique feature set (translations, password recovery) mentioned in the context. It then enforces Wide Aggregation by requiring the agent to traverse the associated product hierarchy to find specific, scattered software versions (iPadOS/watchOS/visionOS 26) and hardware models (Ultra 3, 17 Pro) that belong to the same fictional release cycle.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Apple Site Map About Apple Apple Leadership Career Opportunities Investors Contact Apple Legal Choose Your Country or Region Product Feedback Apple Values Environment Supply C
rounds.answer_a:
macOS Sequoia (macOS 15), unveiled at WWDC 2024, is the specific release featuring a dedicated Passwords app that allows users to recover deleted passwords for 30 days before permanent removal [1]. The "fresh new design" and automatic translation capabilities for calls and messages were highlighted in the parallel iOS 18 announcement, representing the same integrated software generation [2]. The corresponding operating systems are iPadOS 18, which extends these translation features and design refinements to tablets with enhanced customization [3]; watchOS 11, delivering intelligent activity tracking and smart stack improvements [4]; and visionOS 2, introducing spatial photo transformations and ultrawide Mac virtual display capabilities [5]. The designated Ultra wearable model is the Apple Watch Ultra 2, featuring a rugged 49mm titanium case and advanced dual-frequency GPS [6]. The Pro iPhone model is the iPhone 16 Pro, distinguished by its A18 Pro chip, Camera Control button, and pro-grade camera system [7]. These coordinated releases form Apple's unified 2024 ecosystem, delivering consistent password management, translation services, and interface updates across all devices.

## References
[1] https://www.apple.com/macos/macos-sequoia-preview/
[2] https://www.apple.com/ios/ios-18-preview/
[3] https://www.apple.com/ipados/ipados-18-preview/
[4] https://www.apple.com/watchos/watchos-11-preview/
[5] https://www.apple.com/visionos/visionos-2-preview/
[6] https://www.apple.com/apple-watch-ultra-2/
[7] https://www.apple.com/iphone-16-pro/
rounds.answer_b:
The macOS release described is macOS Sequoia (version 15). This update introduces a "fresh new design" for the desktop, iPhone Mirroring, automatic translation for calls and messages in supported apps like Messages and FaceTime, and a new Passwords app with deleted password recovery [1][2].

The associated operating system versions in this same software generation (2024) across the ecosystem are:
*   **iPad:** iPadOS 18 [1][2]
*   **Apple Watch:** watchOS 11 [1][2]
*   **Apple Vision Pro:** visionOS 2 [1]

Within the hardware lineup for this generation, the relevant "Ultra" wearable is the **Apple Watch Ultra 2** (the most current "Ultra" model as of this release) [1][3]. The corresponding "Pro" iPhone model is the **iPhone 15 Pro** (the flagship "Pro" model released alongside these software announcements) [1][3].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. (2024). *Apple introduces macOS Sequoia*. Apple Newsroom. https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2024/06/apple-introduces-macos-sequoia/
[2] Apple Inc. (2024). *macOS Sequoia Preview*. Apple. https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/
[3] Apple Inc. (2024). *Apple Event – September 2023*. YouTube. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6bqLc0sYz8w

Once this operating system version is identified, provide a summary that includes the new operational capabilities listed for Spotlight, the specific automatic language features added to calls and messages, and the stated functionality for recovering deleted credentials.

It then demands **Wide Information Aggregation** to extract specific details across three different functional areas (Spotlight, Communication, Security) found within the source text.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
macOS Sequoia (version 15) is explicitly described as featuring a "fresh new design" while introducing iPhone Mirroring to view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop alongside integrated Apple Intelligence capabilities [1].

Spotlight received enhanced operational capabilities including richer previews that appear directly beneath the search field, showing detailed information without opening files. Users can now perform calculations, unit conversions, and access web-based information more seamlessly. Live results provide real-time data from sports, stocks, and weather, while improved navigation allows quick access to system settings and features through natural language queries [2].

For calls and messages, macOS Sequoia added automatic real-time transcription of audio messages and voicemail, with Apple Intelligence enabling automatically generated summaries of message threads and call transcripts. The system also introduces real-time translation capabilities during calls through a redesigned Phone app accessible on Mac, and intelligent reply suggestions that contextually adapt based on message content [3].

Deleted credentials recovery functionality allows users to retrieve recently deleted passwords from Settings > Passwords for up to 30 days. Deleted credentials move to a "Recently Deleted" folder where they remain recoverable before permanent removal, with iCloud Keychain syncing this recovery capability across all Apple devices [4].

## References
[1] Apple. (2024). "macOS Sequoia." Apple.com.  
[2] Apple. (2024). "macOS User Guide: Search with Spotlight." Support.apple.com.  
[3] Apple. (2024). "Apple Intelligence features in macOS." Apple.com.  
[4] Apple. (2024). "Recover deleted passwords on Mac." Support.apple.com.
rounds.answer_b:
macOS Sequoia is the operating system described as featuring a “fresh new design” and the ability to “view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop” with integrated Apple Intelligence [1][2]. Its new capabilities significantly enhance productivity and system intelligence.

The new operational capabilities for Spotlight include faster, more relevant search results and a redesigned design that surfaces information from apps, contacts, and the web more quickly [1]. It also provides a natural language search for asking complex questions, thanks to deeper integration with Apple Intelligence [2].

For calls and messages, macOS Sequoia introduces specific automatic language features. Live Audio Transcription automatically creates transcripts of audio recordings and meetings, allowing for easy review and searching [1][2]. Live Speech enables users to type what they want to say and have it spoken aloud during calls, FaceTime, or in-person conversations [1].

The stated functionality for recovering deleted credentials is provided by the new Passwords app. This dedicated app, accessible from the Dock or menu bar, organizes passwords, verification codes, and security alerts. Crucially, it features a “Recently Deleted” folder, allowing users to recover passwords they have accidentally removed within the last 30 days [1][2].

## References
[1] Apple. (2024). macOS Sequoia Preview. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/
[2] Apple. (2024). macOS Sequoia: Features Available in Beta. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/features/
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents correctly identified the target entity as macOS Sequoia (Note: The Ground Truth 'macOS Tahoe' appears to be a hallucination or error in the prompt itself, as the features described—iPhone Mirroring and Apple Intelligence—are exclusive to macOS Sequoia). 

Agent B wins on Accuracy and Utility regarding the 'Credentials' constraint. macOS Sequoia introduced a standalone **Passwords App**, which Agent B correctly identified. Agent A provided outdated instructions ('Settings > Passwords'), which is factually incorrect for this specific OS version and would confuse a user looking for the new app. 

While Agent A was more comprehensive on the 'Spotlight' features, the hallucination of the old settings path for passwords in Agent A is a significant negative user experience issue for a 'User Guide' query. Agent B is safer and more accurate regarding the specific OS changes requested.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question: According to the Mac User Guide for the operating system that incorporates 'Apple Intelligence', what specific functionality allows users to operate their iPhone directly from the computer's desktop, and what mechanism is provided within the privacy and security settings to restore accidentally removed authentication credentials?
checklist_width: Functionality to 'View and control' the iPhone directly from the Mac desktop
Ability to 'copy and paste' between the Mac and the other device (iPhone)
checklist_depth: Identification of the OS context featuring 'Apple Intelligence' (Corresponds to source text's macOS Tahoe / Real world macOS Sequoia)
Connection of 'iPhone operation' to the 'Use your Mac with other Apple devices' section
Connection of 'Restoring credentials' to the 'Control your privacy and security' section
rounds.rationale: The question uses deep reasoning by filtering for the specific OS version that includes 'Apple Intelligence' (masking the specific name 'Sequoia' or the source text's 'Tahoe'). It requires wide aggregation by demanding details from two distinct, unconnected sections of the guide: the 'Use your Mac with other Apple devices' section (iPhone Mirroring) and the 'Control your privacy and security' section (Password recovery).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
macOS Sequoia incorporates iPhone Mirroring, which enables users to view and control their iPhone directly from the Mac desktop through a dedicated window. This functionality displays the iPhone's Lock Screen and Home Screen, allowing full interaction with apps, notifications, and content using the Mac's keyboard, trackpad, and mouse. Continuity features ensure the iPhone remains locked during mirroring, preserving security while enabling seamless drag-and-drop file transfers and clipboard sharing between devices [1].

For credential recovery, the operating system provides a "Recently Deleted" folder within Settings > Passwords. When authentication credentials are accidentally removed, they are transferred to this folder and remain recoverable for 30 days before permanent deletion. Users can restore individual passwords or select multiple credentials simultaneously via the "Edit" function. The recovery mechanism integrates with iCloud Keychain, ensuring deleted passwords remain restorable across all synchronized Apple devices through the same Privacy & Security settings [2].

## References
[1] Apple. (2024). "Use iPhone Mirroring on your Mac." Support.apple.com.  
[2] Apple. (2024). "Recover deleted passwords on your Mac." Support.apple.com.
rounds.answer_b:
The specific functionality is called iPhone Mirroring, a feature of macOS Sequoia. It allows users to view and fully interact with their iPhone screen directly from their Mac desktop, even while the iPhone is locked or in another room. The iPhone’s screen appears as a separate window on the Mac, and users can use their Mac’s keyboard, trackpad, and mouse to operate the iPhone app, drag and drop files between devices, and receive iPhone notifications on the Mac. This functionality requires the iPhone to be on the same Wi-Fi network and signed into the same Apple ID with two-factor authentication enabled. It leverages Continuity features for a seamless integration [1][2].

The mechanism provided to restore accidentally removed authentication credentials is the "Recently Deleted" folder within the new Passwords app. This dedicated application, accessible from the Dock or menu bar in macOS Sequoia, serves as a central vault for passwords, passkeys, and verification codes. When a user deletes a login credential, it is not immediately permanently erased. Instead, it is moved to this "Recently Deleted" folder where it remains recoverable for a period of 30 days. During this window, users can browse this folder, select the accidentally removed password or passkey, and restore it to their main passwords list with a simple action. This provides a crucial safety net, ensuring users can recover from accidental deletions without permanent loss of access [1][2]. This feature is part of the enhanced privacy and security framework in macOS Sequoia, which also includes the new Passwords app replacing the legacy Keychain Access utility for a more user-friendly and secure management experience [2].

## References
[1] Apple. (2024). macOS Sequoia Preview. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/
[2] Apple Support. (2024). Use iPhone Mirroring on Mac. Retrieved from https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/mchl7d6c0d2f/mac
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identifies that in macOS Sequoia (the OS with Apple Intelligence), password management has moved to a standalone 'Passwords app', whereas Agent A incorrectly cites the legacy path 'Settings > Passwords' found in previous macOS versions. Agent B also explicitly mentions 'passkeys' as requested in the checklist. Both agents failed to use bolding or lists for better readability, preventing a 'Much Better' score.
